title: GameDevMind: Shortcut Sprint
description: Route the GameDevMind through flashing development paths by replaying pulse sequences and compressing familiar problems into risky shortcut swipes.

howToPlayJson.title: GameDevMind: Shortcut Sprint
howToPlayJson.objective: Keep the GameDevMind build flowing for a 2-4 minute run by memorizing pulse routes across development nodes, replaying them on beat, and cashing in shortcut opportunities before 3 crashes end the session.

controls.input: Tap the glowing development node in the shown order
controls.outcome: Advances the sequence and builds combo

controls.input: Swipe across two linked nodes marked as a Shortcut instead of tapping both
controls.outcome: Skips a repeated pair, saves time, and boosts multiplier if correct

controls.input: Hold on the current node for a brief beat when a Sync Ring appears
controls.outcome: Stabilizes the tempo but pauses multiplier growth for that step

steps: Watch a short pulse route travel through a compact GameDevMind map of development nodes like Code, Art, Audio, QA, and Launch.
Replay the exact route by tapping the same nodes in order while matching their audio cues.
Every few seconds, one repeated node pair becomes a marked Known Problem; choose whether to tap it safely or swipe it as a compressed Shortc
Success adds a new beat, speeds the preview slightly, and raises feedback intensity with sharper sounds and screen shake.
A wrong input or missed beat causes a crash; after 3 crashes, the run ends and your score can enter the local leaderboard.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Each correct node is worth 10 points. Perfect beat timing adds 5. Combo starts at x1 and climbs to x5 every 6 correct inputs without a crash. A correct Shortcut swipe adds a flat 40 points and preserves combo, but a bad
tips: Listen first: each development node has its own tone, so the melody is easier to remember than the colors alone.
Use Shortcut swipes only on patterns you truly recognize; safe taps are slower but protect the run.
When the map starts speeding up, watch the route shape between nodes instead of individual flashes.
